items.statement: The `nproc invocation` --ignore= option if possible, exclude this number of processing units.
items.source_quote_or_summary: If possible, exclude this number of processing units.

items.statement: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.
items.source_quote_or_summary: Print the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.

items.statement: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.
items.source_quote_or_summary: If this information is not accessible, then print the number of processors installed.

items.statement: If the OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables are set, then they will determine the minimum and maximum returned value respectively.
items.source_quote_or_summary: If the OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables are set, then they will determine the minimum and maximum returned value respectively.

items.statement: Linux cgroup version 2 CPU quotas may also limit the maximum returned value.
items.source_quote_or_summary: Linux cgroup version 2 CPU quotas may also limit the maximum returned value.

items.statement: The result is guaranteed to be greater than zero.
items.source_quote_or_summary: The result is guaranteed to be greater than zero.

items.statement: The program accepts the following options.
items.source_quote_or_summary: The program accepts the following options.

items.statement: Print the number of installed processors on the system, which may be greater than the number online or available to the current process.
items.source_quote_or_summary: Print the number of installed processors on the system, which may be greater than the number online or available to the current process.

items.statement: The OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables, or cgroup CPU quotas, are not honored in this case.
items.source_quote_or_summary: The OMP_NUM_THREADS or OMP_THREAD_LIMIT environment variables, or cgroup CPU quotas, are not honored in this case.

items.statement: If possible, exclude this number of processing units.
items.source_quote_or_summary: If possible, exclude this number of processing units.

items.statement: An exit status of zero indicates success, and a nonzero value indicates failure.
items.source_quote_or_summary: An exit status of zero indicates success, and a nonzero value indicates failure.

items.statement: # Linux Processes Official Sources Recommended official pages: - GNU Coreutils manual: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/coreutils.html - `timeout`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/timeout-invocation.html - `nohup`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nohup-invocation.html - `nice`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nice-invocation.html - `sleep`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/sleep-invocation.html - `nproc`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nproc-invocation.html - `signal(7)`: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/signal.7.html Shared references: - `../_shared/intro(1) - Linux manual page.html` - `../_shared/Linux man pages online.html` Note: - This category covers process control, timing, and signal-related basics.
items.source_quote_or_summary: # Linux Processes Official Sources Recommended official pages: - GNU Coreutils manual: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/coreutils.html - `timeout`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/timeout-invocation.html - `nohup`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nohup-invocation.html - `nice`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nice-invocation.html - `sleep`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/sleep-invocation.html - `nproc`: https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/nproc-invocation.html - `signal(7)`: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/signal.7.html Shared references: - `../_shared/intro(1) - Linux manual page.html` - `../_shared/Linux man pages online.html` Note: - This category covers process control, timing, and signal-related basics.

items.statement: - `signal(7)` comes from the Linux man-pages project and is useful for Linux-specific process behavior.
items.source_quote_or_summary: - `signal(7)` comes from the Linux man-pages project and is useful for Linux-specific process behavior.
